title: Mildly Fluffy Syrian Barramundi Tagine
description: Nutty, fluffy, and cozy, this Syrian dish features barramundi and is easy to make in a clay tajine vessel with a deglazing step for added flavor.
instructions: Heat butter and olive oil in a large clay tajine over medium heat.
Add chopped onion to the tajine and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add paprika, cumin, coriander, salt, and fluff salt. Stir to combine.
Add chopped parsley, almonds, lemon juice, water, degling liquid, and vegetable broth to the tajine. Stir to combine.
Add barramundi fillets to the tajine, skin side up.
Cover the tajine and cook until the barramundi is cooked through, about 8 minutes.
Remove the cover and increase the heat to high. Cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the liquid has reduced and the barramundi is slightly browned on the bottom.
Fluff the rice with a fork and stir in rice flour. Serve over the barramundi, garnished with additional parsley and almonds if desired.
Use caution when removing the hot tajine from the stovetop and serving.
Clean the tajine thoroughly after use to prevent staining.
Store any leftovers in a covered container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
